description: Cross-version expected differences on the synthetic IV bolus dataset (S001, C0_true=100 ng/mL, k=0.15 1/h, dose=100 mg, no t=0 observation). All values engine-computed. All three versions (5.3, 6.4, 8.3) use identical algorithm defaults: auc_method='linear', c0_method='log_back_extrap', output_pred_variants=True. Results are numerically identical across versions.

differences.reason: All versions use 'log_back_extrap' (two-point back-extrapolation to t=0), recovering C0_true~100. WNL 5.3 p.196 and WNL 6.4/8.3 UG confirm identical algorithm.
All versions use 'linear' (Linear Trapezoidal Linear Interpolation) as the default AUC method. WNL 5.3/6.4/8.3 UG all confirm 'linear' as the NCA Settings default.
Same AUC method and C0 anchor across all versions produces identical AUMClast.

WNL 5.3 includes the Tmax data point in Lambda_z candidate windows ('Points prior to Cmax'); WNL 6.4/8.3 explicitly exclude it ('and the point at Cmax for non-bolus models'). This propagates to all Lambda_z-derived parameters.

WNL 5.3 includes the Tmax data point in Lambda_z candidate windows ('Points prior to Cmax'); WNL 6.4/8.3 explicitly exclude it ('and the point at Cmax for non-bolus models'). This propagates to all Lambda_z-derived parameters.

WNL 5.3 includes the Tmax data point in Lambda_z candidate windows ('Points prior to Cmax'); WNL 6.4/8.3 explicitly exclude it ('and the point at Cmax for non-bolus models'). This propagates to all Lambda_z-derived parameters.
CL = Dose/AUCINF_obs. Since AUCINF_obs is identical across versions, CL is identical.

WNL 5.3 includes the Tmax data point in Lambda_z candidate windows ('Points prior to Cmax'); WNL 6.4/8.3 explicitly exclude it ('and the point at Cmax for non-bolus models'). This propagates to all Lambda_z-derived parameters.
R^2 of terminal log-linear regression is independent of AUC/C0 methods.
Adjusted R^2 same reasoning as Rsq.
Cmax is the maximum observed concentration — identical across versions.
Clast is the last observed quantifiable concentration — identical across versions.
